rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 11, 2025 Line 12. DEFENDANTs RONALD FOREMAN,and FOREMAN & BRASSO Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Continued to April 15, 2025, per amended notice of petition, filed 3/24/25. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAINIE S. WOOTEN VS. FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 11, 2025 Line 3. DEFENDANT FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS's Motion To Stay. (Amended) Defendant Five Keys Schools and Programs' motion to stay based on the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is GRANTED IN PART. The court stays this case until July 10, 2025, to enable defendant to file a motion to consolidate in the related case (Rainie S. Wooten v. Five Keys Schools and Programs, Case No. CGC-23-606322). Plaintiff Rainie S. Wooten's arguments regarding different parties (the State) and different causes of action are unpersuasive. The underlying violations in each case are the same and individual suits may result in conflicting judgments. "In keeping with both the practical nature of the rule, and the historically flexible remedial powers of equity, exactitude was not required. That the parties in the two actions 'are not entirely identical' and 'that the remedies sought by the two actions are not precisely the same' is not controlling." (Franklin & Franklin v. 7-Eleven owners for Fair Franchising (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 1168, 1175.) Defendant did not waive the right to bring this motion by not filing a notice of related case and the court also has an interest in judicial economy and avoiding conflicting rulings. If the department presently hearing the putative class action (department 606) denies consolidation, then Defendant may renew its application for a complete stay. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: FREDERIC HUTCHINS MOLL VS. JUDITH M. BRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 11, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANT JUDITH BRASS, AND TRUSTEE OF THE JUDITH M. BRASS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 25, 2012 Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection, And Sanctions. Defendant Judith Brass's Motion To Compel Further Site Inspection And For Sanctions is GRANTED. On February 3, 2025 Defendant Judith M. Brass issued a demand for inspection of property to Plaintiff Frederic Hutchins Moll, for the home located at 3746 Clay Street. The inspection was to include the alleged damage to the "interior and exterior" of the premises. Plaintiff failed to timely object. The parties agreed the inspection was to occur on February 26, 2025. On February 19, 2025, Plaintiff testified at deposition that he had seen cracks in the kitchen and dining room and had noticed other cracks in the house but could not be specific as to which rooms. On the date of inspection, Plaintiff limited the inspection to the garage. Plaintiff refused access to the interior of the home, despite agreeing without objection, to the inspection of the alleged interior damage. Defendant's meet and confer, which consisted of counsel pleading with Plaintiff to allow interior access on the inspection date and Plaintiff telling him to file a motion, was reasonable in light of the record as to this discovery and the impending trial date. Further efforts to meet and confer would be futile. The request at issue is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant Judith M. Brass has shown Plaintiff Frederic Hutchins Moll's site inspection was incomplete. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that any objection was timely and meritorious. The court orders Plaintiff to comply with a further site inspection of the interior of the home. Plaintiff shall provide access on or before April 25, 2025 at 12:00 p.m. to the interior of the home located at 3746 Clay Street, for site inspection of the kitchen and dining room and any other interior area of the home in which Plaintiff alleges damage. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that his actions were substantially justified. Plaintiff shall pay to Defendant $4,000 in sanctions within 10 days of notice of order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: LINDA L. HINKEL VS. PRIMAX INVESTMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Inspection, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 11, 2025 Line 11. PLAINTIFF LINDA HINKEL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Inspection, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Linda L. Hinkel's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Demand for Inspection and for sanctions, is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate that she made a "reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion." (CCP 2016.040.) Plaintiff failed to attach as an exhibit, the meet-and-confer emails between the parties. Rather, Plaintiff provides the purported text of the emails in his counsel declaration. The provided email text was conclusory and did not establish a good faith attempt to informally resolve any specific issues now raised in the motion. For instance, Plaintiff's counsel began the communication with "What on earth is that!? How about the documents I requested?" When Defendant's counsel responded and asked for a letter with more specific information, Plaintiff refused to send a written letter. Further, after Defendant's counsel provided documents on March 8, 2025, Plaintiff made no further attempts to meet and confer. Such a record simply does not reflect a reasonable and good faith attempt to informally resolve each issue now presented. The record must reflect such efforts before the court involves itself in resolving the disputes for the parties. The record does not show Plaintiff acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Plaintiff and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $500 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by April 28, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 4, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
